question: Move the balloon-holding girl from the left to sit on the chair in the lower right corner, change the balloon to green, and change her raincoat to yellow.
Move the leftmost painting to the far right position, shift the rightmost painting to the center, and relocate the center painting to the leftmost position.
Shrink the swan slightly and reposition it to the lower-left corner of the image.
Resize the picnic basket in the center to a normal, realistic size.
Move the father from the far right to stand directly behind the grandson, making the grouping more compact.
Make the butterfly take flight, moving it upward and away from the flower petals.
Zoom out a bit, I want to see the full extent of the kitchen island.
Tidy up the items on the desk, stacking all the books neatly in the upper right corner.
Move the airplane lower in the frame so it appears to be passing directly through the gap between the two buildings.
Rearrange all three paintings: move the large one to the right side, swap the vertical positions of the two smaller paintings (top becomes bottom, bottom becomes top), and place them on the left.
Make the car smaller to create the impression that it is farther from the camera.
Elongate the model's figure to make her legs appear longer, while keeping the background steps and architectural lines perfectly straight and undistorted.
Remove the referee and move the two boxers closer together to create an intense, confrontational face-off.
Rearrange the composition by aligning all tech devices in a straight horizontal line from left to right, ordered by size from smallest to largest.
Transform this overhead shot into an eye-level first-person perspective, as if I'm seated at one end of the table looking across toward the opposite side
Change the camera angle to an extreme low-angle shot from ground level—like the dramatic effect of crouching down and gazing up at the cathedral to make it appear monumentally imposing

Horizontally mirror-flip the entire image from left to right, but ensure all English text—the neon sign, chalk menu board, and newspaper headlines—remains correctly oriented and fully readable, not reversed
Change this to a street-level pedestrian perspective, as if I'm standing on the road looking up at the towering skyscrapers on both sides

I'd like to swap the living area and dining area from left to right—move the sofa, coffee table, floor lamp, and gallery wall to the right side, and relocate the dining table, chairs, and pendant lamp to the left side. Please rearrange this for me.

The boardwalk looks too cluttered with people spread everywhere. Please gather all the hikers toward the front section of the boardwalk (lower portion of the frame), leaving the back half of the trail completely empty
Move the viewpoint about two to three meters to the right of the stone arch, as if I walked a few steps to the right before taking the photo

Expand the canvas on both the left and right sides, then move dad and mom out from the back row to stand beside the grandparents on either side, arranging everyone in a single horizontal row instead of having front and back rows.
Shrink the model in the black blazer on the right by approximately 20%, then shift them slightly to the right to create more breathing room between the two figures so they're not crowded together
Rotate the entire image counterclockwise by approximately 15 degrees, but fill in all four corner gaps created by the tilt with appropriate content - no black borders allowed

Move the central bronze sculpture forward (toward the camera) by approximately 6 feet and rotate it clockwise by roughly 15°. The sculpture's material and color must remain unchanged; its reflection and shadow on the marble floor must shift accordingly. Correctly update occlusion relationships with the people behind: any visitors or background elements previously hidden by the sculpture that would now become visible must be seamlessly filled in without visible artifacts.

I want to add more depth to the image: create the effect of the camera shifting half a meter to the right (not simple cropping or stretching), the perspective of the train and platform should change accordingly as a whole; people on the right near the camera should appear closer and slightly larger, while the crowd on the left should appear relatively more distant. All text on signage must not become distorted or blurry, and the repeating tile texture on the walls must remain natural without visible seams or artifacts.

Arrange the 5 spice jars into a neat, orderly row: lids facing up, labels facing directly toward the camera, with perfectly even spacing between each jar (as if using an 'align and distribute evenly' function). Do not alter the size proportions of any jar; keep the shadows intact, and naturally fill in the exposed countertop speckled texture where the jars originally sat.

Move the airborne basketball closer toward the hoop so it appears about to drop in, simultaneously shift the defender on the right who is reaching up to block about half a body width to the right, and raise him into a full jump with both feet off the ground, positioning his palm closer to the ball without touching it. The floor reflections and shadows beneath this player must track and align with his new position; keep the net and all other players' positions unchanged, and ensure the crowd in the stands does not become blurry or distorted.

The model is currently standing too centered: shift the model approximately one step toward the left side of the frame, and move her slightly closer to the camera (making her appear roughly 5–8% larger than before), while keeping her pose, arm angles, and facial expression completely unchanged. The shadow positions on the backdrop paper, floor tape marks, and cable occlusion relationships must all be realistically updated to reflect the new position; do not move the lights or the photographer.

Move the lead singer from the right side to the center of the frame while keeping the other band members in their original positions. The microphone cable must naturally extend to connect with its original routing on the stage floor; do not distort or alter the audience's raised smartphones. Fill the singer's original position naturally with stage haze and ambient lighting.

Make the orange tent in the center more of a 'hero element': first scale it up by approximately 15%, then shift it slightly backward (appearing a bit farther from the campfire), while still keeping it positioned between the two blue tents. The contact edge where the tent meets the ground, the gravel and fallen leaf texture, and the direction of the campfire's light and shadows on the tent must all remain realistic—no floating tent or stretched ground artifacts.

I want the image to look as if viewed slightly from above: shift the overall perspective as though the camera has been raised approximately 20–30 centimeters with a gentle downward tilt, so the easel, tabletop, and floor perspective all adjust accordingly; however, keep the painter and cat in their current positions with their sizes largely unchanged (no stretching or warping), maintain the consistent direction of window light, and ensure the newspapers and brushes on the ground don't become oddly distorted.

Relocate the shopping cart from its mid-right position to the front-left corner of the frame, moving it closer to the camera (scale up to approximately 120% of original size). The cart wheels must realistically align with the tile grout lines, and floor reflections must update accordingly; the vacated area requires seamless inpainting of shelves and floor, and text on product packaging must remain sharp without warping or smearing.
Change the camera angle/perspective so I can clearly read the flight numbers and gate assignments on the large departure display board.
Add one player to the team on the left side of the frame so that both teams have an equal number of players. Raise the camera viewpoint so that the net poles only occupy half the frame height.
